member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEClockOffset.ClockDrift
member.summary: Specifies whether to adjust clock drifts between machines

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.AdjustClockOffset
member.summary: Sets and gets the flag on whether to adjust all time arguments for the clock drift between the ACE server and the PI Server. The default value is True.
member.remarks: When retrieving PI events, carrying out summary calculations, or sending data to PI, a time argument, be it explicitly specified or implicitly set to its default value, may be corrected for the clock drift between the ACE server and the PI Server. The clock drift is defined to be the difference in UTC seconds between two clocks at the same time (i.e., it does not depend on time-zone difference between the two clocks). Note that this flag does not affect the events retrieved from PI (e.g., via PrevEvent and PIValues methods) as they are not corrected for the clock drift.

example.code: myTag.AdjustClockOffset = False

member.summary: Returns the acknowledgement status code for an alarm tag at the current execution time.

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.ArcMode
member.summary: Sets and gets the archive mode. The default value is dmErrorDuplicates.
member.returns:
Returns a value from the DataMergeConstants enumeration. Possible values are:
dmErrorDuplicates
dmInsertDuplicates
dmReplaceDuplicates
dmReplaceOnlyDuplicates

remarks.para: Currently only dmReplaceDuplicates is supported. All other values are treated as dmErrorDuplicates.
With ArcMod set to dmInsertDuplicates, no archive value is replaced.
With ArcMode set to dmReplaceDuplicates, an archive value with the same timestamp is replaced.
Setting ArcMode to dmReplaceDuplicates bypasses the exception reporting process and may result in significant performance degradation. Use it with care.

example.code: myOutputTag.ArcMode = dmReplaceDuplicates

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.Avg(System.Object,System.Object,System.Double)
member.summary: Determines the time-weighted average of archived values for the point over a given time interval.
member.param: Timestamp for the beginning of the time range.
Timestamp for the end of the time range.
[Optional] Minimum time percentage over the given time range, that the point’s archived values must be good.
member.returns: Time-weighted average of archived values for the point over a given time interval.

remarks.para: If the point has no good values or the percentage of good values in the specified time interval is less than PctGood, the method returns Nothing and raises an error.

para.note: If the point has few good archive values during the time period, this function’s result may not be trustworthy. Use the PctGood function to find out the percentage of the values that are good.

example.code: TempVar = Sinusoid.Avg(DateTime.Now.AddDays(-0.1), DateTime.Now) TempVar = Sinusoid.Avg("*-1d", "*", 80)

example.code: Dim intDSCode as Integer intDSCode = cdm158.Code() 'Returns the digital state code associated with the value of cdm158 tag at current execution time intDSCode = sinusoid.Code() 'Returns -1 (assuming sinusoid.Value() is not a digital state)

member.param: Timestamp.

example.code: Dim intDSCode as Integer intDSCode = cdm158.Code("*-2h") 'Returns the digital state code associated with the value of cdm158 tag two hours before the current execution time intDSCode = sinusoid.Code("*-2h") 'Returns -1 (assusming sinusoid.Value("*-2h") is not a digital state)

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.DigState(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the digital state object for a specified string.
member.param: String corresponding to a digital state.
member.returns: Digial state object corresponding to the specified string if successful and Nothing if otherwise.

remarks.para: For a digital point, the digital state is obtained based on first the digital state table for the point and, if not found, the system digital state table.
For non-digital points, the digital state is generated based on the system digital state table.
If there is no digital state corresponding to the specified string, then Nothing is returned, and an error is raised.

example.code: Dim TempVar As PISDK.DigitalState Dim strName As String Dim intCode As Integer TempVar = sinusoid.DigState("Shutdown") strName = TempVar.Name intCode = TempVar.Code TempVar = cdm158.DigState("Auto")

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.ExeTime
member.summary: Sets and returns the current execution time in UTC seconds.
member.returns: Current execution time in UTC seconds.

para.note: The execution time is automatically set when calculations are carried out and is used as the default timestamp for retrieving data from and writing data to PI. Rarely should the users set its value.

remarks.para: In general, the current execution time differs from the current time (i.e., the Now() function in Visual Basic). Some of the reasons include the delay of calculations due to resource constraints. For natural-scheduled calculations, the execution time, which equals to the event time for a PI tag, generally differs from the time when an ACE Module is notified about this new event. Furthermore, there is an intentional latency to execute calculations after receiving an event.

example.code: Dim TempVar As Double TempVar = cdm158.ExeTime TempVar = Sinusoid.ExeTime - 60

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.FindEQ(System.Object,System.Object,System.Object)
member.summary: Finds the first time, within a specified time range, when an archive value for the point is equal to a given value.
member.param: Timestamp for the beginning of the time range to search.
Timestamp for the end of the time range to search. This time may be earlier than Starttime; if so, the range is searched backwards.
The value to search for. Must be an integer or real number, or character string corresponding to a digital state.
member.returns: PITime object corresponding to the first timestamp, within a specified time range, when an archive value for the point is equal to a specified target value.
member.remarks: If the point does not contain any value that meets the criteria, returns Nothing and raises an error.

example.code: Dim TempVar As PITimeServer.PITime TempVar = sinusoid.FindEQ(DateTime.Now.AddHours(-1), DateTime.Now, "Shutdown") TempVar = cdm158.FindEQ("*-1h", "*", "Auto")

member.param: Timestamp for the beginning of the time range to search.
Timestamp for the end of the time range to search. This time may be earlier than Starttime; if so, the range is searched backwards.
The value to search for. Must be an integer or real number, or character string corresponding to a digital state.
member.returns: PITime object corresponding to the first timestamp, within a specified time range, when an archive value for the point is greater than or equal to a specified target value.
member.remarks: If the point does not contain any value that meets the criteria, returns Nothing and raises an error.

example.code: Dim TempVar As PITimeServer.PITime TempVar = sinusoid.FindGE(DateTime.Now.AddHours(-1), DateTime.Now, "Shutdown") TempVar = cdm158.FindGE("*-1h", "*", "Auto")

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.IsGood
member.summary: Determines if the value for the tag at the current exetime equals one of the system digital states.
member.returns: Returns True if a value is good and False otherwise.

remarks.para: A value is considered bad if it equals one of the system digital states. False is returned if the point does not contain any value at or before the specified timestamp, or if the requested event could not be retrieved from the Server.

para.note: One should avoid using a Boolean in arithmetic as the behavior is implementation dependent. For example, True is converted to -1 in VB while it is 1 for PE.

example.code: Dim TempVar As Boolean TempVar = Sinusoid.IsGood() TempVar = Cdm158.IsGood()

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.IsGood(System.Object)
member.summary: Determines if the value for the tag at the specified timestamp equals one of the system digital states.
member.param: Timestamp.
member.returns: Returns True if a value is good and False otherwise.

remarks.para: A value is considered bad if it equals one of the system digital states. False is returned if the point does not contain any value at or before the specified timestamp, or if the requested event could not be retrieved from the Server.

para.note: One should avoid using a Boolean in arithmetic as the behavior is implementation dependent. For example, True is converted to -1 in VB while it is 1 for PE.

example.code: Dim TempVar As Boolean TempVar = Sinusoid.IsGood(DateTime.Today) TempVar = Cdm158.IsGood(Cdm158.ExeTime)

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.IsQuestionable
member.summary: Sets or returns whether the current output value is questionable. The default is False (i.e., not questionable).
member.returns: Returns True if a value is questionable and False otherwise.

remarks.para: This flag is only applicable to output tags and its setting applies only to the current value. The flag is reset to its default value (i.e., False) when the timestamp is reset.

example.code: myOutputTag.IsQuestionable = True

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.IsRecalculating
member.summary: Returns if the current calculation is auto or manual recalculation.
member.returns: True if current calculation is auto or manual recalculation.

remarks.note: Users should not set this property.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.IsSet(System.String)
member.summary: Determines if value for the tag at current execution time is annotated, substituted, or questionable.
member.param:
A string but only the first character is considered. Possible values are:
"a" for annotated
"s" for substituted
"q" for questionable It is case-insensitive.
member.returns: True if value for the tag at the current execution time has the specified quality attribute; False otherwise.
member.remarks: Returns False if an invalid quality string is specified and an error is raised.

example.code: TempVar = Sinusoid.IsSet("a")

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.IsSet(System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Determines if value for the tag at the specified time is annotated, substituted, or questionable.
member.param:
A string but only the first character is considered. Possible values are:
"a" for annotated
"s" for substituted
"q" for questionable It is case-insensitive.
Timestamp.
member.returns: True if value for the tag at the specified time has the specified quality attribute; False otherwise.
member.remarks: Returns False if an invalid quality string is specified and an error is raised.

example.code: TempVar = Sinusoid.IsSet("a")

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.IsTrigger
member.summary: Determines if the tag triggered the current calculation.
member.returns: Returns True if the tag triggered the current calculation and False if otherwise.

remarks.para: This function is only applicable for scheduled (clock or natural) calculation events and always returns False for recalculations events.

para.note: A calculation may be triggered by multiple trigger tags. The function accesses a cache of one hundred triggering events per tag. If the calculation is severely delayed (e.g., the calculation has been triggered by the tag more than a hundred times but none of the calculations has been executed), this function may return the wrong status.

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.IsAlias
member.summary: Determines if the tag corresponds to a PIAlias.
member.returns: True if the tag corresponds to a PIAlias and False otherwise.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.Max(System.Object,System.Object,System.Double)
member.summary: Maximum archive value of the point over a given time interval.
member.param: Timestamp for the beginning of the time range.
Timestamp for the end of the time range.
[Optional] Minimum time percentage (double) over the given time range, that the point’s archived values must be good.
member.returns: Returns the maximum archive value of the point over a given time interval.
member.remarks: If the point has no good values or the PctGood minimum is not met for the given time range, returns Nothing and raises an error.

example.code: Dim TempVar as Double TempVar = Sinusoid.Max(DateTime.Now.AddHours(-1), DateTime.Now) TempVar = Sinusoid.Max(DateTime.Now.AddHours(-1), DateTime.Now 80)

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.Mean(System.Object,System.Object,System.Double)
member.summary: Average value of all archive events in the interval.
member.param: Timestamp for the beginning of the time range.
Timestamp for the end of the time range.
[Optional] Minimum time percentage (double) over the given time range, that the point’s archived values must be good.
member.returns: Returns the average value of all archive events in the interval.
member.remarks:
If the point has no good values or the PctGood minimum is not met for the given time range, returns Nothing and raises an error.
Care should be taken when calling this method, as the events are not time-weighted. Use Avg for time weighting.

example.code: Dim TempVar as Double TempVar = Sinusoid.Mean(DateTime.Now.AddHours(-1), DateTime.Now) TempVar = Sinusoid.Mean(DateTime.Now.AddHours(-1), DateTime.Now 80)

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.Min(System.Object,System.Object,System.Double)
member.summary: Minimum archive value of the point over a given time interval.
member.param: Timestamp for the beginning of the time range.
Timestamp for the end of the time range.
[Optional] Minimum time percentage (double) over the given time range, that the point’s archived values must be good.
member.returns: Returns the minimum archive value of the point over a given time interval.
member.remarks: If the point has no good values or the PctGood minimum is not met for the given time range, returns Nothing and raises an error.

example.code: Dim TempVar as Double TempVar = Sinusoid.Min(DateTime.Now.AddHours(-1), DateTime.Now) TempVar = Sinusoid.Min(DateTime.Now.AddHours(-1), DateTime.Now 80)

member.remarks: If the point has no archive data after the specified time, this function returns Nothing.

member.returns: Value (as an Object) of the point’s next archive event after a specified time.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.PctGood(System.Object,System.Object)
member.summary: Returns the percentage of time, over a given time period, that the point’s archived values are good.
member.param: Timestamp for the beginning of the time range.
Timestamp for the end of the time range.
member.returns: Returns the percentage of time, over a given time period, that the point’s archived values are good.
member.remarks: If an error occurs in retrieving values from archive, returns Nothing and raises an error.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.PointType
member.summary: Returns the point’s type.
member.returns:
Returns one of the following values from the PISDK.PointTypeConstants enumeration as the point's type:
6 (Int16)
8 (Int32)
11 (Float16)
12 (Float32)
13 (Float64)
101 (Digital)
102 (Blob)
105 (String)

example.code: Dim TempVar as Integer TempVar = Sinusoid.PointType TempVar = Cdm158.PointType

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.PrevEvent
member.summary: Returns timestamp of the point’s previous archive event before the current execution time.
member.returns: PITime object corresponding to the timestamp of the point’s previous archive event before the current execution time.
member.remarks:
If the point has no archive data before the current execution time, this function returns Nothing.
Due to the exception reporting and compression, the last archived event generally differs from the last snapshot event. PrevEvent returns the timestamp for the previous archived event. See the product manual (Chapter: Advanced Topics) for discussion on a method for obtaining the timestamp for the last snapshot event.

example.code: Dim TempVar as PITimeServer.PITime TempVar = Sinusoid.PrevEvent() TempVar = Cdm158.PrevEvent()

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.PrevEvent(System.Object)
member.summary: Returns timestamp of the point’s previous archive event before a specified time.
member.param: Timestamp.
member.returns: PITime object corresponding to the timestamp of the point’s previous archive event before a specified time.
member.remarks:
If the point has no archive data before the specified time, this function returns Nothing.
Due to the exception reporting and compression, the last archived event generally differs from the last snapshot event. PrevEvent returns the timestamp for the previous archived event. See the product manual (Chapter: Advanced Topics) for discussion on a method for obtaining the timestamp for the last snapshot event.

example.code: Dim TempVar as PITimeServer.PITime TempVar = Sinusoid.PrevEvent(DateTime.Now.AddDays(-0.1)) TempVar = Cdm158.PrevEvent("*-1h")

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.PrevVal
member.summary: Returns value of the point’s previous archive event before the current execution time.
member.returns: Value (as an Object) of the point’s previous archive event before the current execution time.
member.remarks:
If the point has no archive data before the current execution time, this function returns Nothing.
Due to the exception reporting and compression, the last archived value generally differs from the last snapshot value. PrevVal returns the previous archived value. See the product manual (Chapter: Advanced Topics) for discussion on a method for obtaining the last snapshot value.

example.code: Dim TempVar as Object TempVar = Sinusoid.PrevVal()

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.PrevVal(System.Object)
member.summary: Returns value of the point’s previous archive event before a specified time.
member.param: Timestamp.
member.returns: Value (as an Object) of the point’s previous archive event before a specified time.
member.remarks:
If the point has no archive data before the specified time, this function returns Nothing.
Due to the exception reporting and compression, the last archived value generally differs from the last snapshot value. PrevVal returns the previous archived value. See the product manual (Chapter: Advanced Topics) for discussion on a method for obtaining the last snapshot value.

example.code: Dim TempVar as Object TempVar = Sinusoid.PrevVal(DateTime.Now.AddDays(-0.1))

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.PutValue
member.summary: Sends the value for an output tag to the PI Snapshot Subsystem.
member.remarks: Users should not normally attempt to call this method themselves.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.Range(System.Object,System.Object,System.Double)
member.summary: Returns the difference between the point’s maximum and minimum archive values during a specified time interval.
member.param: Timestamp for the beginning of the time range.
Timestamp for the end of the time range.
[Optional] Minimum time percentage over the given time range that the point’s archived values must be good.
member.returns: Returns the difference between the point’s maximum and minimum archive values during a specified time interval.
member.remarks: If the point has no good values or the time percentage of archive values for the specified time range that is good is less than PctGood, returns Nothing and raises an error.

example.code: Dim TempVar as Double TempVar = Sinusoid.Range(DateTime.Now.AddDays(-1), DateTime.Now) TempVar = Sinusoid.Range(DateTime.Now.AddDays(-1), DateTime.Now, 80)

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.RefreshAttribute
member.summary: Refreshes tag attributes immediately.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.ResetCurrentValue
member.summary: Resets the currently cached value for the point.

remarks.para: For an input tag, the currently cached value is the snapshot or archive value retrieved when the Value method is called or equals the last value set through the Value method. For an output tag, the currently cached value is the last value set through the Value method.
The timestamp associated with the currently cached value equals either the execution time or the last time specified for the Value method.
When the Value method is called with a different timestamp, the currently cached value is automatically updated.

example.code: Debug.Print Sinusoid.Value (=40 for example) Sinusoid.Value = Sinusoid.Value + 10 Debug.Print Sinusoid.Value (=50) Sinusoid.ResetCurrentValue() Debug.Print Sinusoid.Value (=40)

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.ResultTimestamp
member.summary: Returns the timestamp associated with the current value.
member.returns: PITime object corresponding to the timestamp associated with the current value of the tag.
member.remarks: This property applies to an output or input/output point only and returns Nothing for an input point. In the former case, it returns the timestamp associated with the last value set. If the last value set has no timestamp specified, it defaults to current execution time. The timestamp is overwritten whenever you set a new value.

example.code: Dim TempVar as PITimeServer.PITime TempVar = Sinusoid.ResultTimeStamp()

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.SendDataToPI
member.summary: Sets and returns if the current value for the point should be sent to PI.
member.remarks: The default is to send every value for an output tag to PI. Setting this value applies only to one execution time.

example.code: Dim TempVar as Boolean TempVar = Cdm158.SendDataToPI Sinusoid.SendDataToPI = False

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.Server
member.summary: Returns the server name where the point is defined.
member.returns: String representing the name of the server where the point is defined.

example.code: Dim TempVar as String TempVar = Cdm158.Server

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.SetTag(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Sets the context and tag alias for a PIACEPoint object, to be used as an input tag.
member.param: A string consisting of the full PIModule path or the server name. The delimiter separating two PIModules is a backslash ( \ ). For example:\\MyPIServer\ParentPIModule\ChildModule.

A string for a tag or PIAlias. If Context is a PIModule path, then the tag corresponding to the PIAlias in the PIAliases collection of the specified PIModule is used. If no such PIAlias exists, the tag whose name is AliasName is used. If no tag exists, then no tag is used and an error is raised.
member.remarks: When defining a PIACEPoint object, it is necessary to call the SetTag method so that other calls can be resolved.

example.code: Dim MyInputTag As PIACEPoint MyInputTag = New PIACEPoint() MyInputTag.SetTag("MyPIServer", "MyInputTag")

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.SetTag(System.String,System.String,OSIsoft.PI.ACE.TagAliasUsedType)
member.summary: Sets the context and tag alias for a PIACEPoint object.
member.param: A string consisting of the full PIModule path or the server name. The delimiter separating two PIModules is a backslash ( \ ). For example:\\MyPIServer\ParentPIModule\ChildModule.

A string for a tag or PIAlias. If Context is a PIModule path, then the tag corresponding to the PIAlias in the PIAliases collection of the specified PIModule is used. If no such PIAlias exists, the tag whose name is AliasName is used. If no tag exists, then no tag is used and an error is raised.

Value from TagAliasUsedType enumeration specifying whether the tag or alias is used as an input, output or both. Possible values are:
TagAliasAsInput = 1
TagAliasAsOutput = 2
TagAliasAsBoth = 3
member.remarks: When defining a PIACEPoint object, it is necessary to call the SetTag method so that other calls can be resolved.

example.code: Dim MyInputTag As PIACEPoint MyInputTag = New PIACEPoint() MyInputTag.SetTag("MyPIServer", "MyInputTag", TagAliasAsOutput)

member.summary: Returns the PI SDK string point source.
member.returns: String corresponding to the PI SDK point source.

example.code: Dim TempVar as String TempVar = Cdm158.Source

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.Span
member.summary: Returns the point’s span.
member.returns: Returns the point’s span.

example.code: Dim TempVar as Double TempVar = Cdm158.Span

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.StateNo(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the digital state code for a specified string.
member.param: String for a digital state.
member.returns: Returns an integer corresponding to the digital state code for a specified string.
member.remarks: The digital state is obtained based on the digital state table for the point. If the digital state table does not contain a digital state with the corresponding string, the method returns Nothing and raises an error. For non-digital points, the method always returns Nothing and raises an error.

example.code: Dim TempVar as Integer TempVar = Sinusoid.StateNo("Calc Failed") (returns Nothing if Sinusoid is a numeric point and an error is raised.) TempVar = Cmd158.StateNo("Manual")

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.StDev(System.Object,System.Object,System.Double)
member.summary: Returns the time-weighted standard deviation of archive values for the point over a given time interval.
member.param: Timestamp for the beginning of the time range.
Timestamp for the end of the time range.
[Optional] Minimum time percentage over the given time range that the point’s archived values must be good.
member.returns: Returns the time-weighted standard deviation of archive values for the point over a given time interval.
member.remarks: If the point has no good values or the PctGood minimum is not reached for the given time range, returns Nothing and raises an error.

example.code: Dim TempVar as Double TempVar = Sinusoid.StDev(DateTime.Now.AddDays(-1), DateTime.Now) TempVar = Sinusoid.StDev(DateTime.Now.AddDays(-1), DateTime.Now, 80)

member.summary: Returns the tag used as type (i.e., as Input, Output, or Both).
member.returns:
Possible return values are:

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.TimeEQ(System.Object,System.Object,System.Object)
member.summary: Finds the total time in seconds, within a specified time range, when archive values for the point are equal to a given value.
member.param: Timestamp for the beginning of the time range to search.
Timestamp for the end of the time range to search. This time may be earlier than Starttime; if so, the range is searched backwards.
The value to search for. Must be an integer or real number, or character string corresponding to a digital state.
member.returns: Total time in seconds, within a specified time range, when archive values for the point are equal to the specified target value.
member.remarks: If the point does not contain any value that meets the criteria, returns 0.0.

example.code: Dim TempVar As Double TempVar = sinusoid.TimeEQ(DateTime.Now.AddHours(-1), DateTime.Now, "Shutdown") TempVar = cdm158.TimeEQ("*-1h", "*", "Auto")

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.TimeLE(System.Object,System.Object,System.Object)
member.summary: Finds the total time in seconds, within a specified time range, when archive values for the point are less than or equal to a given value.
member.param: Timestamp for the beginning of the time range to search.
Timestamp for the end of the time range to search. This time may be earlier than Starttime; if so, the range is searched backwards.
The value to search for. Must be an integer or real number, or character string corresponding to a digital state.
member.returns: Total time in seconds, within a specified time range, when archive values for the point are less than or equal to the specified target value.
member.remarks:
If the point does not contain any value that meets the criteria, returns 0.0.
This function interpolates between archive events, if necessary, to find the times when the point crossed the given value.

example.code: Dim TempVar As Double TempVar = Cdm158.TimeLE(Now.AddDays(-1), Now, "Manual") TempVar = Sinusoid.TimeLE(Now.AddDays(-1), Now, 55)

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.TimeLT(System.Object,System.Object,System.Object)
member.summary: Finds the total time in seconds, within a specified time range, when archive values for the point are less than a given value.
member.param: Timestamp for the beginning of the time range to search.
Timestamp for the end of the time range to search. This time may be earlier than Starttime; if so, the range is searched backwards.
The value to search for. Must be an integer or real number, or character string corresponding to a digital state.
member.returns: Total time in seconds, within a specified time range, when archive values for the point are less than the specified target value.
member.remarks:
If the point does not contain any value that meets the criteria, returns 0.0.
This function interpolates between archive events, if necessary, to find the times when the point crossed the given value.

example.code: Dim TempVar As Double TempVar = Sinusoid.TimeLT(Now.AddDays(-1), Now, 55)

member.returns: Total time in seconds, within a specified time range, when archive values for the point are greater than or equal to the specified target value.

If the point does not contain any value that meets the criteria, returns 0.0.

example.code: Dim TempVar As Double TempVar = Cdm158.TimeGE(Now.AddDays(-1), Now, "Manual") TempVar = Sinusoid.TimeGE(Now.AddDays(-1), Now, 55)

member.summary: Finds the total time in seconds, within a specified time range, when archive values for the point are greater than a given value.

Timestamp for the end of the time range to search. This time may be earlier than Starttime; if so, the range is searched backwards.
The value to search for. Must be an integer or real number, or character string corresponding to a digital state.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.TimeNE(System.Object,System.Object,System.Object)
member.summary: Finds the total time in seconds, within a specified time range, when archive values for the point are not equal to a given value.
member.param: Timestamp for the beginning of the time range to search.
Timestamp for the end of the time range to search. This time may be earlier than Starttime; if so, the range is searched backwards.
The value to search for. Must be an integer or real number, or character string corresponding to a digital state.
member.returns: Total time in seconds, within a specified time range, when archive values for the point are not equal to the specified target value.
member.remarks: If the point does not contain any value that meets the criteria, returns 0.0.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.Total(System.Object,System.Object,System.Double)
member.summary: Returns the totalized value (time integral) in unit per day of archive values for the point over a given time interval.
member.param: Timestamp for the beginning of the time range.
Timestamp for the end of the time range.
[Optional] Minimum time percentage over the given time range, that the point’s archived values must be good.
member.returns: Totalized value (time integral) in unit per day of archive values for the point over a given time interval.

remarks.para: If the point has no good values or the percentage of good values in the specified time interval is less than PctGood, the method returns Nothing and raises an error.

para.note: If the point has few good archive values during the time period, this function’s result may not be trustworthy. Use the PctGood function to find out the percentage of the values that are good

remarks.para: The system chooses a scale factor such that the integral will be correct only if the flow is expressed in units per day. If the flow is expressed in units per hour, or per some other time unit, you should multiply this result by a conversion factor. The conversion factor equals the number of actual flow time units in a day.

example.code: Dim TempVar as Double TempVar = Sinusoid.Total(DateTime.Now.AddDays(-0.1), DateTime.Now) TempVar = Sinusoid.Total("*-1d", "*", 80)

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.TypVal
member.summary: Returns the point's typical value.
member.returns: Returns an Object corresponding to the point's typical value.

example.code: Dim TempVar as Object TempVar = Sinusoid.TypVal()

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.Value
member.summary: Sets or returns the value at the current execution time for the point.
member.returns: Value (as an Object) for the tag at the current execution time.

remarks.para: For an output tag, this property does not retrieve data from PI. For an input tag, it always retrieves data from PI the first time it is called and the data is cached. Since this property can be used to store any intermediate value, its value may differ from the snapshot or archive value. The following example illustrates this point:
remarks.code: Debug.Print sinusoid.Value() (=23.45 for example) sinusoid.Value() = sinusoid.Value() + 10 Debug.Print sinusoid.Value() (=33.45)

example.code: Dim TempVar as Object TempVar = Sinusoid.Value() TempVar = Cdm158.Value() Sinusoid.Value() = 10.0 Cmd158.Value() = "Auto"

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.Value(System.Object)
member.summary: Sets or returns the value at a specified time for the point.
member.param: Timestamp specified in any valid time formats (e.g. Date, UTC Seconds, or string according to relative or absolute PI Time format).
member.returns: Value (as an Object) for the tag at the specified time.

remarks.para: For an output tag, this property does not retrieve data from PI. For an input tag, it always retrieves data from PI the first time it is called and the data is cached. Since this property can be used to store any intermediate value, its value may differ from the snapshot or archive value. The following example illustrates this point:
remarks.code: Dim TimeStamp As Date TimeStamp = DateTime.Now Debug.Print sinusoid.Value(TimeStamp) (=23.45 for example) sinusoid.Value(TimeStamp) = sinusoid.Value(TimeStamp) + 10 Debug.Print sinusoid.Value(TimeStamp) (=33.45)

example.code: Dim TempVar as Object TempVar = Sinusoid.Value("*-1h") TempVar = Cdm158.Value(DateTime.Now.AddDays(-0.1)) Sinusoid.Value("*-1h") = 10.0 Cmd158.Value("1-1-2001 1:13AM") = "Auto"

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.CachedSnapshot
member.summary: Returns the snapshot event in the cache.
member.returns: PIValue for the snapshot event in the cache.
member.remarks: The snapshot in the cache may be different from the snapshot on the PI server.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.Values(System.Object,System.Object)
member.summary: Returns all events for a specified time range from the archive as a PIValues collection.
member.param: Timestamp for the beginning of the time range.
Timestamp for the end of the time range.
member.returns: PIValues collection consisting of all events for a specified time range from the archive.
member.remarks: For points with the Step attribute set (i.e., Step = 1), this function returns boundary values at or within the time range specified. For points without the Step attribute set (i.e., Step = 0), this functions returns boundary values interpolated to the start and end times specified.

example.code: Dim TempVar as PISDK.PIValues TempVar = Sinusoid.Values(DateTime.Now.AddDays(-1), DateTime.Now)

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint.Values(System.Object,System.Object,PISDK.BoundaryTypeConstants)
member.summary: Returns all events for a specified time range from the archive as a PIValues collection.
member.param: Timestamp for the beginning of the time range.
Timestamp for the end of the time range.

An enumerated type (0, 1, 2, and 3) that determines how the boundary points are returned. Possible values are:
0: To return boundary values at or within the time range specified.
1: To return boundary values at or outside the time range specified.
2: To return boundary values interpolated to the start and end times specified.
3: To return boundary values at or within the time range specified for points with the Step attribute set (i.e., Step = 1) and to return boundary values interpolated to the start and end times specified for points without the Step attribute set (i.e., Step = 0).
member.returns: PIValues collection consisting of all events for a specified time range from the archive.

example.code: Dim TempVar as PISDK.PIValues TempVar = Sinusoid.Values(DateTime.Now.AddDays(-1), DateTime.Now, 2)

member.returns: Returns the point's zero.

member.name: T:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEPoint
member.summary: Every PI Tag or Alias is declared as a PIACEPoint object within an ACE Module.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.ACos(System.Double)
member.summary: Returns the inverse (or arc) cosine of a number.
member.param: A real number between -1.0 and 1.0.
member.returns: Inverse (or arc) cosine of a number.
member.remarks: If value is a number smaller than -1.0 or greater than 1.0, an error is raised.

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.AdjustClockDrift
member.summary: Sets and returns if ACE should adjust clock drifts between the ACE machine and PI servers.
member.value: True implies adjust for clock drift between ACE machine and PI Servers; False disables clock drift adjustment for all calculations within an ACE executable.
member.returns: True if adjusting for clock drift between ACE machine and PI Servers and False otherwise.
member.remarks: The default value for AdjustClockDrift is True. It applies to all calculations within an ACE Executable. One should be careful when changing the default value: If the ACE Machine is ahead of a PI Server, you may try to retrieve a value in the future and may get the "No Data" event.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.ASin(System.Double)
member.summary: Returns the inverse (or arc) sine of a number.
member.param: A real number between -1.0 and 1.0.
member.returns: Inverse (or arc) sine of a number.
member.remarks: If value is a number smaller than -1.0 or greater than 1.0, an error is raised.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Atn2(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Returns the inverse (or arc) tangent of a pair of numbers, which represent a point on the plane.
member.param: A real number representing the x-axis coordinate.
A real number representing the y-axis coordinate..
member.returns: Inverse (or arc) tangent of a pair of numbers. If you draw a line between the point whose Cartesian coordinates are (x, y) and the origin, the angle between that line and the x-axis is the inverse tangent of y/x.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Chars(System.Object[])
member.summary: Returns a string from ASCII character codes.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.ClearLastPIACEError
member.summary: Clears the last error message generated by ACE library functions.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Compare(System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Compares two strings.
member.param: First input string.
Second input string. "*" and "?" in the second string are treated as wild characters.
[Optional] Boolean flag indicating if the comparison is case sensitive.
member.returns: True if the input strings are the same and False if otherwise.
member.example: Compare("?","a") returns False Compare("What","wh") returns False Compare("What","wha?") returns True Compare("What","wh*") returns True Compare("What","wh*", 1) returns False

member.returns: String concatenating input strings.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Cosh(System.Double)
member.summary: Returns the hyperbolic cosine of an angle.
member.param: An angle, measured in radians.
member.returns: Hyperbolic cosine of value.
member.remarks: The angle, value, must be in radians. Multiply by Math.PI/180 to convert degrees to radians.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Curve(System.Double,System.Object,System.Object)
member.summary: Returns the y value of a curve given the x value.
member.param: A numeric value where the curve needs to be evaluated.
An array of numeric values representing the x-coordinates of the curve.
An array of numeric values representing the y-coordinates of the curve.
member.returns: Returns the y value of a curve given the x value.
member.remarks: The function does not extrapolate and the xs should be sorted with strictly increasing order. Returns Nothing and raises an error if there is at least one non-numeric value in xs or ys, and if their dimensions do not match.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.DaySec(System.Object)
member.summary: Returns the number of seconds from midnight to a specified time.
member.param: Timestamp.
member.returns: Number of seconds from midnight to a specified time.

example.code: Dim TempVar as Double TempVar = DaySec(Sinusoid.ExeTime) TempVar = DaySec(DateTime.Now)

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.DigState(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the digital state corresponding to a character string representing the digital state.
member.param: A string representing a digital state.
member.returns: PISDK.DigitalState object corresponding to a character string representing the digital state.
member.remarks: This function searches the system digital state table on the default PI Server from the PISDK known servers table.If the character string does not represent a digital state in the system table, returns Nothing.

example.code: Dim MyDigState as PISDK.DigitalState MyDigState = DigState("digitalstring")

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.DigState(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Returns the digital state corresponding to a character string representing the digital state.
member.param: A string representing a digital state.
The PI Server name whose system digital state table is searched for the specified digital state.
member.returns: PISDK.DigitalState object corresponding to a character string representing the digital state.
member.remarks: If the character string does not represent a digital state in the system table, returns Nothing.

example.code: Dim MyDigState as PISDK.DigitalState MyDigState = DigState("digitalstring", "MyPIServer")

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.FONM
member.summary: Returns the timestamp for the midnight at the first of the next month.
member.returns: PITime object corresponding to midnight at the first of the next month.

example.code: Dim TempVar as PITimeServer.PITime TempVar = FONM()

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.FONM(System.Object)
member.summary: Returns the timestamp for the midnight at the first of the month next to a specified time.
member.param: Timestamp.
member.returns: PITime object corresponding to midnight at the first of the month next to a specified time.

example.code: Dim TempVar as PITimeServer.PITime TempVar = FONM("*-20d")

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Frac(System.Object)
member.summary: Returns the fractional part of a real number.
member.param: A real number.
member.returns: Fractional part of a real number.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.GetLastPIACEError
member.summary: Gets the last error message generated by ACE library functions.
member.returns: String representing last error message generated by ACE library functions.

example.code: Dim TempVar as String TempVar = GetLastPIACEError()

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.GetPIModuleFromPath(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the PIModule for a given path.
member.param: String indicating PIModule path. The format should be [ServerName\]PIMod1\PIMod2\…\PIModN.
member.returns: PIModule object corresponding to the specified path.
member.remarks: The specified path should contain a valid server name.

example.code: Dim TempVar As PISDK.PIModule TempVar = GetPIModuleFromPath("MyPIServer\Aquariums\Aquarium1")

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.GetPIModuleFromPath(System.String,PISDK.PIModule)
member.summary: Gets the PIModule for a given path below a root PIModule.
member.param: String indicating PIModule path. The format should be [ServerName\]PIMod1\PIMod2\…\PIModN.
The root PIModule object.
member.returns: PIModule object corresponding to the specified path below the root PIModule.

example.code: Dim TempVar As PISDK.PIModule Dim RootPIModule As PISDK.PIModule RootPIModule = GetPIModuleFromPath("MyPIServer\Aquariums") TempVar = GetPIModuleFromPath("Aquarium1", RootPIModule)

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.InRange(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Determines if a value is in the specified range.
member.param: Real number that needs to be checked if in range.
A real number representing a lower bound of the specified range.
A real number representing an upper bound of the specified range.
member.returns: True if value is in range; False otherwise.
member.remarks: If lowerbound is larger than upperbound, their values are switched.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.InSet(System.Object,System.Object[])
member.summary: Determines if a value (a number or string) equals to the specified set of values.
member.param: Input value (number or string) that needs to be checked if equals the specified set of values.
x1, x2, .., xN:Values (numeric or string) representing a set.
member.returns: True if the value is in set; False otherwise.
member.example: InSet(1, "1", 2, 3) returns True InSet("3", 1, 2, 3) returns True InSet("What?", "about", 1, 2) returns False

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.IsDST
member.summary: Determines if current time is in a daylight saving time (DST) period on the local machine.
member.returns: True if the current time is in a DST period, False otherwise.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.IsDST(System.Object)
member.summary: Determines if a specified time is in a daylight saving time (DST) period on the local machine.
member.param: Timestamp.
member.returns: True if the specified time is in a DST period, False otherwise.
member.remarks: An error is raised if the specified time is invalid.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Log10(System.Double)
member.summary: Returns the base 10 logarithm of a specified number.
member.param: A number whose logarithm is to be found.
member.returns:
Possible values are:
The base 10 log of value for positive value.
Double.NegativeInfinity for value=0.
Double.NaN for negative value.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.LogLastPIACEError(System.Int32,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Logs the last error message generated by ACE library functions.
member.param: Error number.
Location where the error occurs.
[Optional] Detailed error description.
[Optional] Offending expression causing error.

example.code: LogLastPIACEError(-12034, "MyProgram.MySub", "Invalid number: Should be between 0 and 5", "intTest = 6") ClearLastPIACEError()

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.LogPIACEMessage(OSIsoft.PI.ACE.MessageLevel,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Logs a message to the PI message system.
member.param: Type of message to be sent and the relevant ones are: mlErrors, mlWarning, mlChange, and mlUserMessage.
A message string.
Full ACE Context path. Should be set to the Name property of the base class (MyBase.Name).

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Max(System.Object[])
member.summary: Determines the maximum from the specified arguments.
member.param: x1, x2, .., xN: Input arguments.
member.returns: Maximum value amongst the specified arguments.
member.remarks: Non-numerical arguments are ignored in the evaluation. An error may be raised if there is no numeric argument.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Median(System.Object[])
member.summary: Returns the median (middle) value of all arguments.
member.param: x1, x2, .., xN: Input arguments.
member.returns: Median value of all input arguments.
member.remarks: If the number of arguments is even, the average of the two middle values is returned. An error is raised for non-numeric arguments.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Min(System.Object[])
member.summary: Determines the minimum from the specified arguments.
member.param: x1, x2, .., xN: Input arguments.
member.returns: Minimum value amongst the specified arguments.
member.remarks: Non-numerical arguments are ignored in the evaluation. An error may be raised if there is no numeric argument.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Noon
member.summary: Determines timestamp for the noon on the current day.
member.returns: PITime object corresponding to the timestamp for the noon on the current day.

example.code: Dim TempVar as PITimeServer.PITime TempVar = Noon()

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Noon(System.Object)
member.summary: Determines timestamp for the noon on the day of a specified time.
member.param: Timestamp.
member.returns: PITime object corresponding to the timestamp for the noon on the day of a specified time.

example.code: Dim TempVar as PITimeServer.PITime TempVar = Noon("*-2d") TempVar = Noon(DateTime.Now.AddDays(-2))

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.ParseTime(System.String)
member.summary: Returns a PITime object for a given time evaluated with respect to the current time.
member.param: A timestamp string (in time/date, absolute or relative PI time formats).
member.returns: PITime object corresponding to the specified time.

example.code: Dim myPITime as PITimeServer.PITime myPITime = ParseTime("14-Dec-97") myPITime = ParseTime("t") 'The specified absolute PI time "t" is evaluated with respect to the current time. myPITime = ParseTime("*-1h") 'The specified relative PI time "*-1h" is evaluated with respect to the current time.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.ParseTime(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Returns a PITime object for a given time evaluated with respect to a specified reference time.
member.param: A timestamp string (in time/date, absolute or relative PI time formats).
A string specifying a reference time. If the parameter time is in the absolute or relative PI time format,it is evaluated with respect to the reference time.
member.returns: PITime object corresponding to the specified time.

example.code: Dim myPITime as PITimeServer.PITime myPITime = ParseTime("t", "*-2d") 'The specified absolute PI time "t" is evaluated with respect to the reference time "*-2d". myPITime = ParseTime("*-1h", "*-2d") 'The specified relative PI time "*-1h" is evaluated with respect to the reference time "*-2d".

member.summary: Represents the day of the month corresponding to current time.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Poly(System.Double,System.Object)
member.summary: Evaluates a polynomial with given coefficients at a specified point.
member.param: Point where the polynomial needs to be evaluated.
An array of coefficients defining the polynomial, starting with the 0-th order term.
member.returns: Value of the polynomial, defined by the the given coefficients, at a specified point x.
member.remarks: If x or any coefficient is not numeric, raises an error.

example.code: Dim coefficients() as Double = {1.0, 2.0, 3.0, 4.0} Dim myVal as Double myVal = Poly(1.0, coefficients) 'Returns 10.0 myVal = Poly(0.0, coefficients) 'Returns 1.0

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.PStDev(System.Object)
member.summary: Determines the population standard deviation of two or more numbers.
member.param: Array of numbers, timestamps, or time periods, but all must be the same.
member.returns: Population standard deviation of input arguments.
member.remarks: Bad values are ignored in computing the standard deviations. If all values are bad, an error is raised.

example.code: Dim TempVar As Double TempVar = PStDev(Array(1,2,3,4)) TempVar = PStDev(Array(DateTime.Now, DateTime.Now.AddDays(-1), DateTime.Now.AddDays(-0.5)))

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Sinh(System.Double)
member.summary: Returns the hyperbolic sine of an angle.
member.param: An angle, measured in radians.
member.returns: Hyperbolic sine of value.
member.remarks: The angle, value, must be in radians. Multiply by Math.PI/180 to convert degrees to radians.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.SStDev(System.Object)
member.summary: Determines the sample standard deviation of two or more numbers.
member.param: Array of numbers, timestamps, or time periods, but all must be the same.
member.returns: Sample standard deviation of input arguments.
member.remarks: Bad values are ignored in computing the standard deviations. If all values are bad, an error is raised.

example.code: Dim TempVar As Double TempVar = SStDev(Array(1,2,3,4)) TempVar = SStDev(Array(DateTime.Now, DateTime.Now.AddDays(-1), DateTime.Now.AddDays(-0.5)))

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Tanh(System.Double)
member.summary: Returns the hyperbolic tangent of an angle.
member.param: An angle, measured in radians.
member.returns: Hyperbolic tangent of value.
member.remarks: The angle, value, must be in radians. Multiply by Math.PI/180 to convert degrees to radians.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Total(System.Object[])
member.summary: Determines the sum of all specified arguments.
member.param: x1, x2, .., xN: Input numbers.
member.returns: Sum of all numeric input arguments.
member.remarks: If an argument is not a number, an error is raised.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Trunc(System.Object)
member.summary: Truncates the specified numeric or timestamp to the next lower integer or second, respectively.
member.param: Numeric or timestamp value that needs to be truncated.
member.returns:
Possible return values are:
Value of type Double truncated to next lower integer for numeric value.
Value of type Date trucated to next lower second for timestamp value.
member.remarks: Returns Nothing and raises an error if value is an invalid time string.

example.code: Dim myDouble As Double Dim myDate As Date myDouble = Trunc(22.5) 'Returns 22 myDate = Truc(DateTime.Today.AddSeconds(0.5)) 'Returns DateTime.Today

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Trunc(System.Object,System.Object)
member.summary: Truncates the specified numeric or timestamp to the next lower unit.
member.param: Numeric or timestamp value that needs to be truncated.
The size of the unit to truncate to. If x is a number, unit must be a number. If x is a timestamp, unit must be a time period.
member.returns:
Possible return values are:
Value of type Double truncated to next lower unit for numeric value.
Value of type Date trucated to next lower unit for timestamp value.
member.remarks: The largest value smaller than value which is an integer multiple of unit is returned. Returns Nothing and raises an error if value is an invalid time string or if unit is of wrong type.

example.code: Dim myDouble As Double Dim myDate As Date myDouble = Trunc(22.5, 5) 'Returns 20 myDate = Truc(DateTime.Today.AddSeconds(7.5),3) 'Returns DateTime.Today.AddSeconds(6)

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.YearDay
member.summary: Represents the day of the year corresponding to the current time.
member.returns: The day of the year, expressed as a value from 1 to 366, where 1 represents January 1.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.YearDay(System.Object)
member.summary: Represents the day of the year corresponding to the specified time.
member.param: Timestamp.
member.returns: The day of the year, expressed as a value from 1 to 366, where 1 represents January 1.

member.name: T:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions
member.summary: Implements various shared (static) functions which can be used from ACE calculations.
member.remarks: When using the shared functions, users can either specify the full paths (e.g., OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions.Trunc()) or use the Imports keyword to import a namespace (e.g., Imports OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACEBIFunctions) and specify the names (e.g., Trunc()).

member.name: T:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.My.Resources.Resources
member.summary: A strongly-typed resource class, for looking up localized strings, etc.

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACENetClassModule.CalculationLimit
member.summary: Sets and returns the maximum amount of time in seconds that a calculation can take.
member.returns: Maximum amount of time in seconds that a calculation can take.
member.remarks: The ACE 2.x scheduler simply aborts any calculations that take longer time than that specified by the Calculation Limit. Subsequent calculation events are not affected.

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACENetClassModule.Context
member.summary: Returns the context under which the ACE module is currently running.
member.returns: String corresponding to the context under which ACE module is running.
member.remarks: This property can be used to perform context specific tasks in a calculation.

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACENetClassModule.ExeTime
member.summary: Current execution time in UTC seconds.
member.returns: Returns the current execution time in UTC seconds.

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACENetClassModule.IsRecalculating
member.summary: Returns if the current calculation is auto or manual recalculation.
member.returns: True if the current calculation is auto or manual recalculation.

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACENetClassModule.IsManualRecalculating
member.summary: Returns if the current calculation is manual recalculation.
member.returns: True if the current calculation is manual recalculation.

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACENetClassModule.Name
member.summary: Returns the full name for the ACE Context.
member.returns: String representing full name of the ACE context (\\server\Exe\Mod\Context).
member.remarks: This property used when calling the LogPIACEMessage method.

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACENetClassModule.MaxQueueSize
member.summary: Sets and returns the maximum number of items on the execution queue.
member.returns: Maximum number of items on the execution queue.
member.remarks: This property represents the number of maximum allowed items on the execution queue associated with an ACE Context. The ACE scheduler starts skipping calculations if the number of events on the execution queue exceeds this limit.

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACENetClassModule.SendDataToPI
member.summary: Sets and returns whether to send results for calculations currently executing to PI.
member.returns: True if sending output tag events to PI; False otherwise.
member.remarks: Each output tag has another flag that determines if its event should be sent to PI. An output tag event is sent to PI if and only if both flags are True.

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACENetClassModule.NumberOfCalculations
member.summary: Returns the total number of calculations executed since the start of the context.
member.returns: Sum of the numbers of aborted calculations, calculations with errors and successful calculations.

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACENetClassModule.NumberOfSkippedCalculations
member.summary: Returns the number of skipped calculations.
member.returns: Number of calculation skipped due to resource constraints.

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACENetClassModule.NumberOfQueuedCalculations
member.summary: Returns the number of queued calculations.
member.returns: The number of items on the queue, waiting to be executed.

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACENetClassModule.NumberOfCalculationsWithError
member.summary: Returns the number of calculations with error.
member.returns: The number of calculations for which exceptions are not caught in the subroutine ACECalculations.

member.name: P:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACENetClassModule.NumberOfAbortedCalculations
member.summary: Returns the number of calculations aborted.
member.returns: The number of calculations which were aborted due to exceeding the calculation limit.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACENetClassModule.InitializePIACEPoints
member.summary: Initializes all PIACEPoint objects.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACENetClassModule.ModuleDependentInitialization
member.summary: Any custom initialization code can be implemented here. Any variable that does not change should be initialized here.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACENetClassModule.ModuleDependentTermination
member.summary: Contains code to dispose any user-defined objects.

member.name: M:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACENetClassModule.ACECalculations
member.summary: This method is called by the Scheduler to execute calculations.

member.name: T:OSIsoft.PI.ACE.PIACENetClassModule
member.summary: A MustInherit base class to be inherited by all ACE Modules.
